keyPoints: Datagram Protocol: Connectionless, best effort delivery, no guarantee of delivery or order of packets
Transmission Control Protocol: Connection-oriented, guarantees delivery and order of packets, error checking and correction
Segments: Breaking down data into smaller packets, each with a sequence number, for efficient routing and reassembly
TCP Connections: Establishing a connection between sender and receiver, using three-way handshake
Flow Control: Preventing network congestion by regulating the amount of data sent at one time
Error Control: Detecting and correcting errors in packets, using checksums and acknowledgement packets
Congestion Control: Managing network congestion by regulating the amount of data sent, using slow-start and congestion avoidance algorithms
